---
title: "Labs"
---

# Labs

A lab is a hands-on learning guide generated from a chat. It takes the conversation context and produces a structured set of steps with an interactive checklist for tracking your progress.

## Generating a lab

From any chat, look for the "Generate Lab" action. Clicking it sends the chat's assembled context to the AI, which produces a structured output containing:

- **Title** — a short name for the lab.
- **Introduction** — context and goals.
- **Steps** — numbered instructions, each rendered as markdown.
- **Checklist** — a list of tasks to complete, each with a checkbox.

The lab is persisted as a leaf artifact on the chat. It does not branch — a lab belongs to exactly one chat.

## The lab runner

The lab view renders the generated content as a scrollable guide:

1. The title and introduction appear at the top.
2. Each step is displayed in order with full markdown rendering (headings, code blocks, lists, images if referenced).
3. Below the steps, the interactive checklist shows each task with a checkbox.

## Interactive checklist

Click any checklist item to toggle its completion state. The progress is saved immediately — close the lab and reopen it later, and your completed items persist.

A progress bar at the top of the checklist shows how many items you have completed out of the total. When all items are checked the lab is marked as complete.

## Progress tracking

The checklist is stored as JSON in the database (`labs.checklist`). Each item has:

- **id** — unique identifier.
- **text** — the task description.
- **done** — boolean completion state.

Because the checklist is persisted in the same local SQLite database as the chat, progress survives app restarts and page reloads. There is no server or account involved.

## Regenerating a lab

If the generated lab does not fit your needs, you can regenerate it. The previous version is replaced with a new generation based on the current chat context. Your checklist progress from the previous version is not carried over.

## Link back to the chat

The lab view includes a link back to its parent chat. This lets you return to the conversation that generated the lab, ask follow-up questions, and generate an updated version.
